channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Playing under the radar: a scoping review to identify evidence gaps in police athletic league research
item.description:
Authors: Daly, Kevin
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose Police Athletic Leagues and Police Activities Leagues (PALs) are widely implemented sports-based youth development programs intended to prevent juvenile delinquency and improve social and developmental outcomes among at-risk youth. Despite their prevalence and substantial public investment, the extent and quality of empirical research evaluating PAL effectiveness remain unclear. This study aims to systema...

item.title: Law enforcement leadership and collaboration with domestic and sexual violence agencies: perspectives from police chiefs and sheriffs
item.description:
Authors: Wengloski & Brubaker
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose Police chiefs and sheriffs have the crucial responsibility of entering their agencies into collaborations with service providers in their community, such as domestic and sexual violence agencies (DSVAs). This study examines how these leaders perceive and make decisions regarding collaborations. Design/metho...

item.title: Police Officers With Supplemental Martial Arts Training Perform Better: A Quasi-Experimental Study With Brazilian Cadets
item.description:
Authors: Del-Colle & Eleuterio-da-Rocha
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

Abstract This study explores the impact of Brazilian jiu-jitsu (BJJ) and Muay Thai (Thai) training on police use-of-force (PUF) performance in a simulated call using a sample of Latin American police. Thirty-four police cadets from a southern Brazilian state were split into three groups based on martial arts experience: BJJ, Thai, and control (no experience). Their performance was evaluated in a simulated use-of-force scenario in terms of tactic...
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Can Contact Improve Police–citizen Relations? Reducing Police Conspiracy Beliefs and Protest Violence
item.description:
Authors: Jolley et al.
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

Abstract Conspiracy beliefs about the police likely strain police-citizens relations, particularly during high-tension encounters such as protests. Understanding how to reduce such beliefs is therefore crucial. Building on evidence that positive intergroup contact can lower intergroup conspiracy beliefs, this research examined how both direct and parasocial contact with the police shape conspiracy beliefs and protest-related...
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: News headline themes and approval of police use-of-force: evidence from a national survey experiment
item.description:
Authors: Mourtgos, Scott M.
Journal: Journal of Experimental Criminology

Abstract Objectives This study tests whether headline themes from news coverage of police use-of-force influence general public approval of legally reasonable police use-of-force. Methods In a between-subjects...

item.title: Organizational and community risk factors for police officer victimization: evidence from Texas municipal police departments
item.description:
Authors: Kim & Zhang
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose This study examines how organizational characteristics and community contexts jointly shape police officer victimization, extending research that has focused mainly on individual encounters. Guided by routine activity theory (RAT) and social disorganization theory (SDT), it considers both institutional capacity and broader environmental conditions. ...

item.title: Networks at Work: Officer Diversity, Network Segregation, and Police Misconduct
item.description:
Authors: Zhao, Linda
Journal: American Journal of Sociology

item.title: The Impact of Prior Military Experience and Burnout on Police Officer Use of Force
item.description:
Authors: Fiske & Schriver
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Cumulative Exposure to Police-Involved Shootings and Mental Health Conditions in Minneapolis, Minnesota
item.description:
Authors: Tanveer et al.
Journal: Journal of Urban Health
